title: Share of the population in poverty vs. Electoral Democracy Index
subtitle: Poverty is defined as living on less than $10 a day. This data is adjusted for inflation and differences in living costs between countries. The electoral democracy index is by [V-Dem](#dod:v-dem), based on expert estimates, and ranges from 0 to 1 (most democratic).
caption:
Share of the population in poverty vs. Electoral Democracy Index

Poverty is defined as living on less than $10 a day. This data is adjusted for inflation and differences in living costs between countries. The electoral democracy index is by V-Dem, based on expert estimates, and ranges from 0 to 1 (most democratic).

The scatter plot displays the relationship between the share of the population living in poverty and the Electoral Democracy Index for various countries in 2025. The vertical axis represents the poverty rate, ranging from 0% to over 80%, while the horizontal axis measures the democracy index from 0 to 1. A general negative trend is visible, where countries with lower democracy scores tend to have higher poverty rates. African nations, marked in purple, such as Burundi, Ethiopia, Benin, and Burkina Faso, cluster in the upper-left quadrant, showing democracy indices between roughly 0.2 and 0.6 and poverty shares exceeding 80%. In contrast, countries in North America and Europe, colored orange and blue respectively, are concentrated in the bottom-right corner. Nations like Australia, Chile, and several European states exhibit democracy indices above 0.8 and poverty shares near or below 5%. Latin American countries, shown in brown, occupy a middle ground; for instance, Peru and Colombia sit between 0.6 and 0.7 on the democracy scale with poverty rates around 40% to 50%, while Brazil and Costa Rica appear further to the right with indices near 0.8 and poverty shares between 10% and 30%. Asian countries display a wide spread: China and Vietnam are positioned on the left with indices around 0.1 to 0.2 and poverty rates near 30%, whereas Malaysia and Thailand are located centrally with indices around 0.4 to 0.5 and poverty shares below 20%. The data is expressed in international dollars at 2021 prices, relating to income or consumption per capita depending on the specific country and year.
